summary: Julia Evans posts a brief note explaining that she has been writing daily blog posts about her time at the Recurse Center but has removed them from her main RSS feed. She feels the daily posts are more stream-of-consciousness than her typical polished writing. She provides links to find the posts in a dedicated category and a separate RSS feed for those interested in following along.
key_points: Evans separates her daily RC posts from her main blog feed to maintain quality expectations for regular subscribers
She characterizes the daily posts as more stream-of-consciousness compared to her usual writing
She provides alternative ways to follow the RC content via category page and dedicated RSS feed
money_quotes: I took them out of this RSS feed because I think they're a bit more stream of consciousness than my usual posts.
key_insight: Separating exploratory, unpolished writing from a curated feed lets you share learning-in-progress without diluting your main body of work.
